name: Amazon
overview: Amazon is the world's largest e-commerce and cloud computing company. Their interview process heavily emphasizes Leadership Principles alongside technical skills.
hiringProcess: Amazon's process includes an Online Assessment, phone screen, and a loop of 4-5 interviews. Each round tests both technical ability and alignment with Amazon's 16 Leadership Principles.
eligibility: Strong problem-solving skills, familiarity with Amazon's Leadership Principles is essential.
preparationTips: Master the STAR method for behavioral questions. Practice LP stories. Focus on system design for senior roles.

interviewQuestions.title: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager
interviewQuestions.content: Amazon tests 'Have Backbone; Disagree and Commit' principle with this question.
interviewQuestions.expectedAnswer: Use STAR method. Show respectful disagreement backed by data, and commitment to the final decision.
interviewQuestions.sampleAnswer: Situation: My manager wanted to use a monolithic architecture for a new service. Task: I believed microservices would be better for our scale. Action: I prepared a comparison document with performance projections and presented it in a team meeting. Result: We adopted a hybrid approach that reduced deployment time by 60%.
followUp: What would you do if your manager still disagreed?
How did this affect your relationship?

interviewQuestions.title: Design an e-commerce recommendation system
interviewQuestions.content: Design a product recommendation engine for Amazon-scale e-commerce.
interviewQuestions.expectedAnswer: Cover collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, real-time vs batch processing, A/B testing framework.
interviewQuestions.sampleAnswer: Use collaborative filtering (user-item matrix) + content-based (product features). Batch compute with Spark, real-time with Kafka streams. Store in Redis for low-latency serving. A/B test with feature flags.
tips: Cover both online and offline components
followUp: How to handle the cold start problem?

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.statement: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

bruteForce.approach: Use a stack to match brackets

optimal.approach: Stack-based matching (same as brute force - already optimal)

problems.interviewTips: Classic stack problem. Mention edge cases: empty string, single character, nested brackets.

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.statement: Given an m x n 2D binary grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands.

bruteForce.approach: DFS from each unvisited land cell

optimal.approach: BFS with queue for iterative approach

problems.interviewTips: Discuss BFS vs DFS trade-offs. BFS is better for large grids to avoid stack overflow.

experiences.lessons: Amazon heavily weighs Leadership Principles. Have 2-3 stories ready for each LP. Use STAR method consistently.
experiences.timeline: Applied → OA (3 days) → Phone (1 week) → Loop (2 weeks) → Offer (1 week)
